TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi sealed SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp extends System.Enum

Docs.summary: Specifies opto-electronic transfer function characteristics as defined by ITU-T H.273 (Coding-Independent Code Points).

remarks.format:
## Remarks
`SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp` enumerates the opto-electronic transfer functions defined by ITU-T H.273 (Coding-Independent Code Points). These values describe the relationship between scene luminance and encoded signal levels for a color space and are used with <xref:SkiaSharp.SKColorSpace.CreateCicp*> alongside <xref:SkiaSharp.SKColorspacePrimariesCicp> to construct a color space from standardized parameters.
This enumeration is a value type and requires no disposal.
## Examples
```csharp // Create a BT.2100 PQ (HDR) color space var colorSpace = SKColorSpace.CreateCicp( SKColorspacePrimariesCicp.Rec2020, SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp.Pq); ```

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Hlg = int32(18)

Docs.summary: ARIB STD-B67 Hybrid Log-Gamma (HLG) transfer function (CICP value 18), used for broadcast HDR video.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Iec6196621 = int32(13)

Docs.summary: IEC 61966-2-1 sRGB transfer function (CICP value 13), the standard gamma curve used for sRGB content on displays.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Iec6196624 = int32(11)

Docs.summary: IEC 61966-2-4 xvYCC transfer function (CICP value 11), used for extended-gamut YCC video.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Linear = int32(8)

Docs.summary: Linear transfer function with no gamma encoding (CICP value 8).

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Pq = int32(16)

Docs.summary: SMPTE ST 2084 Perceptual Quantizer (PQ) transfer function (CICP value 16), used for HDR10 content.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Rec202010bit = int32(14)

Docs.summary: ITU-R BT.2020 10-bit transfer function (CICP value 14), used for 10-bit BT.2020 encoded video.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Rec202012bit = int32(15)

Docs.summary: ITU-R BT.2020 12-bit transfer function (CICP value 15), used for 12-bit BT.2020 encoded video.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Rec470SystemBg = int32(5)

Docs.summary: ITU-R BT.470 System B/G assumed gamma 2.8 transfer function (CICP value 5), used for legacy PAL/SECAM video.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Rec470SystemM = int32(4)

Docs.summary: ITU-R BT.470 System M assumed gamma 2.2 transfer function (CICP value 4), used for legacy NTSC video.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Rec601 = int32(6)

Docs.summary: ITU-R BT.601 transfer function (CICP value 6), used for standard-definition digital video.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Rec709 = int32(1)

Docs.summary: ITU-R BT.709 transfer function (CICP value 1), used for HDTV and sRGB-like encoding.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp SmpteSt240 = int32(7)

Docs.summary: SMPTE ST 240 transfer function (CICP value 7), used in early HDTV production.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp SmpteSt4281 = int32(17)

Docs.summary: SMPTE ST 428-1 gamma 2.6 transfer function (CICP value 17), used for digital cinema DCDM content.

MemberSignature.Value: .field public static literal valuetype SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp Unknown = int32(0)

Docs.summary: Unknown or unspecified transfer function characteristics (CICP value 0).
